episode0.title: THE RECORDINGS — DIRECTOR'S CUT V2 / OPERATION REVISION

puzzle0.title: ARCHIVE DECRYPTION
puzzle0.description: Encrypted archive storage detected. Hale must stay near the terminal while you recover the access key.

event0.text: You saved the Door 12 records. I wish that made me feel better.

event1.text: It doesn't.

event2.text: I'm leaving the relay room now. Archive sector is two decks down, assuming the lifts are still pretending to be dead.

event3.text: Objective is simple: reach the archive, find the missing Incident Alpha package, and leave before the station notices we are interested.

event4.text: I said simple. I did not say sane.

choice0.text: WHAT ARE YOU NOT TELLING ME?

event1.text: I hate that it is fair.

event2.text: I remember deleting something called Incident Alpha. I remember my hand shaking. I do not remember why I pressed confirm.

event3.text: So if I start sounding too certain, do me a favor and don't believe me immediately.

event0.text: Good. Movement first, existential crisis second.

event1.text: I am stepping into the service corridor. Lights are dead on the left side. The right side is doing that charming flicker that makes every shadow look employed.

event2.text: If I start narrating too much, that's not for drama. It's because silence has been a terrible roommate.

event1.text: Junction reached. There are two ways down.

event2.text: Main corridor is faster, open, and full of broken ceiling panels.

event3.text: Service ladder is slower, tighter, and smells like hot plastic. Weirdly, that makes it the more honest option.

event1.text: I'm moving quickly. Not running. Running is how people in training videos become examples.

background1.text: I'm fine. Panel fell behind me. I am aggressively fine.

event2.text: Archive door is ahead. Suit pressure dipped for a second, but it recovered.

event0.text: Service ladder. Sensible. Miserable, but sensible.

event2.text: Found a spare filter clipped inside the shaft. Old, but sealed.

event3.text: I am choosing to interpret that as the station apologizing. Bad apology, useful gift.

event0.text: Archive access door is in front of me.

event1.text: The panel is awake. That is rarely good news. Sleeping panels are lazy. Awake panels have opinions.

event2.text: I can scan it first, or I can force the access panel before the security routine finishes waking up.

event1.text: Good call. There is a quarantine latch on the inner frame. If I had forced it, the archive would have locked itself and probably judged us while doing it.

event2.text: Bypassing latch now. Slowly. Carefully. Two words I have started appreciating very late in life.

event1.text: Door didn't like that.

event2.text: I jammed the quarantine latch, but I got inside. That was technically success. It just arrived wearing bruises.

event3.text: Next time I say a panel is awake, assume it is vindictive.

event1.text: Rows of dead storage columns. Frost on the floor. Someone left this place in a hurry.

event2.text: There. Portable storage device. Still powered.

event3.title: ENCRYPTED STORAGE DEVICE
event3.content:
[ENCRYPTED ARCHIVE FRAGMENT]

Archive Key Hint: ALPHA

Warning: Failed decrypt attempts may trigger quarantine response.

Status: Locked
event3.caption: A portable archive device recovered inside Sector C.

event4.text: It wants a key. Of course it wants a key.

event1.text: Don't guess. The device is not clever. It is bureaucratic. Bureaucracy loves exact wording.

event2.text: I can keep the quarantine loop busy for a moment, but not forever.

choice1.text: ASK HALE TO READ THE DEVICE AGAIN.

event1.text: Archive Key Hint: ALPHA.

event2.text: Not Incident Alpha. Not a clever abbreviation. Just ALPHA.

event3.text: Sometimes terror is hidden behind a label. Which is rude, but efficient.

choice1.text: LET HALE STEADY THE TERMINAL FIRST.

event1.text: My hands were starting to shake. I was pretending they weren't, but apparently the terminal has better eyesight than my pride.

event2.text: Ready. Try it now.

event0.title: INCIDENT ALPHA TRANSCRIPT
event0.content:
21:14 — Door 12 opened.
21:15 — Unidentified movement detected.
21:17 — Security override accepted.
21:19 — Signal interruption.
21:20 — Casualty report created.
21:22 — Archive deletion command issued.
event0.caption: Recovered transcript from the encrypted archive device.
event0.message: [INCIDENT ALPHA TRANSCRIPT ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

event1.text: That recording should not exist.

event2.text: And before you ask— yes, I know how terrible that sentence sounds.

event3.text: 21:22. Archive deletion command issued.

event4.text: I remember a deletion prompt. I do not remember why I accepted it.

choice0.text: WHY DOES THE FILE STILL EXIST?

event0.text: Because either I failed to delete it...

event1.text: or someone wanted me to believe I had.

event2.text: I remember sitting at a terminal. I remember my hand shaking before I confirmed the deletion.

event3.text: I don't remember the reason. That missing reason is starting to feel bigger than the file.

event1.text: There are camera analysis terminals on the next row. If the transcript is true, there should be a visual trace.

event2.text: I would like the visual trace to not be me.

event3.text: I am aware that hope is not a method.

event1.text: Terminal reached. It has the Door 12 feed, but the actual frame is shredded.

event2.text: I can inspect the raw corruption myself, or you can let the station run automated analysis.

event0.text: I hate this plan, which probably means it's useful.

event1.text: There is a suited figure outside Door 12.

event2.text: Engineering outline. Right hand near the manual override.

event3.text: I want to say it isn't me.

event1.text: Honestly, that might be kinder.

event2.text: Running it now.

event0.title: CORRIDOR CAMERA ANALYSIS
event0.content:
SOURCE: Door 12 archive feed
Visual frame unrecoverable.
Automated analysis result:
- One suited figure detected.
- Suit outline compatible with ECHO engineering issue.
- Right-hand object: manual override handle.
- Timestamp damaged.
- Identity confidence: 64%
event0.caption: Automated analysis generated from corrupted camera data. No image recovered.

event1.text: Sixty-four percent.

event2.text: Enough to ruin a life. Not enough to prove one.

event3.text: The system is pointing at me with a broken finger.

event0.text: There should be an override ledger beside the camera terminal.

event1.text: I'm checking it now.

event2.title: SECURITY OVERRIDE LOG
event2.content:
Override User: REDACTED
Authorization: VALID
Source: UNKNOWN
Command Chain: Door 12 / Archive Delete / Emergency Channel Wake
event2.caption: Recovered security ledger for Door 12 and connected commands.
event2.message: [SECURITY OVERRIDE LOG ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

event3.text: Valid authorization. Unknown source.

event4.text: That is not a category. That's a threat wearing a badge.

event1.text: I hate that the honest answer is maybe.

event2.text: But if it was me, why redact myself? Why hide the source and leave the outline?

event3.text: Either I was protecting someone, or someone was building a trail back to me.

event1.text: There. Something rode the emergency channel wake command.

event2.text: It did not open the door. It waited until the door was open.

event3.text: That is worse. I am running out of ways for things to become worse, and yet they remain creative.

event1.text: A deletion command. A Hale-shaped figure. A valid authorization from nowhere.

event2.text: I can trace the deletion chain, or we can open the emergency channel note connected to the wake command.

event3.text: For the record, I dislike both options equally. Very democratic dread.

event1.text: Command issued after casualty report. Before emergency channel wake completed.

event2.text: So whoever deleted Incident Alpha knew someone was dead, and knew the channel was waking up.

event3.text: That makes it less like cleanup and more like containment.

event1.text: I already regret saying that out loud.

event0.title: EMERGENCY CHANNEL NOTE
event0.content:
Emergency Backup Channel

7.14 MHz

Use only if primary communication systems fail.

Warning: Do not open if unauthorized return signal is detected.
event0.caption: Recovered emergency channel instruction connected to Incident Alpha.
event0.message: [EMERGENCY CHANNEL NOTE ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

event2.text: No.

event3.text: Nobody was supposed to find that channel.

event4.text: Backup channels are supposed to wait for you to call them.

event5.text: This one answered before anyone transmitted.

event6.text: That is not a backup. That is a mouth.

event1.text: Thank you for not making me beg. I was prepared to, but I would have hated both of us for it.

event2.text: Operator... the note says not to open it if a return signal already exists.

event3.text: We already have one.

choice1.text: LET HALE REST FOR A MINUTE.

event0.text: THANK YOU FOR OPENING THE DOOR

event1.text: No. Close it. Close it now.

event3.text: Operator, listen to my voice. Not the channel. Mine.

event1.text: There is no such thing as mostly sealed in a healthy system, so let's enjoy what that says about us.

event2.text: We keep moving. Personnel records next. I need to know who else the station thinks is dead.

event1.text: What did you just do?

event2.text: No. Don't answer. I know what you did.

event3.text: Personnel records. Now. Before it learns anything else from us.

event0.text: Personnel records are on the next deck.

event1.text: If Incident Alpha is the wound, personnel is where the station wrote down the body count.

event2.text: I am not ready for that.

event3.text: So obviously we are going.

event1.text: I forgot people can offer those.

event2.text: Okay. I'm sitting against the archive wall. Just for sixty seconds.

event3.text: I can hear the station settling around me. For a second it almost sounds like rain.

choice1.text: ASK ABOUT RAIN WHILE HE MOVES.
